evals.prompt: I have a stack with two branches: feat-auth (parent: main) and feat-auth-tests (parent: feat-auth). Neither has a PR yet. Open PRs for both.
assertions: calls stack-graph.sh list to enumerate branches
calls gh pr create --base main --head feat-auth with a title that describes the change
calls gh pr create --base feat-auth --head feat-auth-tests with a descriptive title
base is the recorded parent, not the repo default
calls stack-graph.sh set to backfill PR numbers
calls safe-push.sh for branches whose tips have moved

evals.prompt: My stack already has PRs open for feat-auth (PR #42) and feat-auth-tests (PR #43). I added a commit to feat-auth. Refresh the PR bodies and push.
assertions: detects existing PRs via gh pr view
does not call gh pr create for branches with existing PRs
calls gh pr edit --body-file - to refresh body for each branch
calls safe-push.sh for feat-auth because its tip moved
does not push feat-auth-tests if its tip is unchanged

evals.prompt: The parent branch feat-auth was squash-merged into main. Now feat-auth-tests shows duplicate commits. I want to submit a PR for feat-auth-tests targeting main.
assertions: recognizes that squash-merge duplicate-commit cleanup is not the job of stack-submit
defers to /stack-reparent for the squash-merge re-parent case
does not attempt to rebase or rewrite history
explains that stack-reparent should be run first before submitting
